nodes.body:
![critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/critical.svg)

This JSON file contains comments, which is not valid according to the JSON specification. This will cause standard JSON parsers to fail. Please remove the comments from this file. If this explanatory text is necessary, it should be moved to a separate documentation file (e.g., a `README.md` in the same directory).

![critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/critical.svg)

This reference JSON file contains comments, which makes it invalid JSON. This will prevent any standard JSON parser or tool from reading it correctly. Please remove the comments. This information could be included in the corresponding `SKILL.md` documentation instead.

![critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/critical.svg)

This file includes `//` style comments, which are not valid in standard JSON and will cause parsing errors for any tool that consumes it. All documentation and explanatory notes should be kept in Markdown files (like the accompanying `SKILL.md`) to ensure JSON files remain syntactically correct and machine-readable.

![security-high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/security-high-priority.svg) ![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The workflow instructs the agent to construct and execute a shell command (`gh pr comment`) using a body that contains untrusted data (summaries of PR comments and changes) without any sanitization or escaping. If an attacker provides a malicious PR comment containing shell metacharacters (e.g., `$(...)`, backticks, or double quotes to break out of the `--body` argument), and the agent includes this content in the summary, the injected commands will be executed on the system where the agent is running. This is a classic Command Injection vulnerability via LLM output.

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The version in `package.json` is set to `1.1.0`, but the pull request title, description, and `CHANGELOG.md` all indicate this is the `1.0.0` release. This inconsistency can lead to confusion and problems with the release process. Please align the version in this file with the intended `1.0.0` release.

```suggestion
  "version": "1.0.0",
```

![security-medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/security-medium-priority.svg) ![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The workflow instructs the agent to execute a GraphQL mutation using the `gh api graphql` command, where the `THREAD_ID` is inserted directly into the command string. While the `THREAD_ID` is expected to come from a previous GraphQL query, if an attacker can influence the IDs returned by the API or if the agent is tricked into using a malicious ID, it could lead to command injection if the ID contains characters that break out of the single-quoted query string.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `zlib` and `createRequire` modules are imported but are not used within the script. It's a good practice to remove unused imports to improve code clarity and maintainability.

```suggestion
import { spawnSync } from 'node:child_process';
```
